title: Pelican to Murder — Chapter 3: The Charity Mirror
description: The investigation moves from sponsor money to public image. A staged livestream, deleted messages, and a garnish-oil handoff reveal how the larger conspiracy uses performance, charity, and social media cover.

answerKey.method: Lured Lena to the photo alcove and triggered her allergy by switching garnish oil
answerKey.motive: To stop Lena from exposing a staged charity livestream scam
answerKey.keyEvidence: Raw livestream cache, garnish bottle photo, and deleted DM thread
answerKey.explanation: Brielle used the livestream chaos to create an alibi while raw cache files placed her near the photo alcove. The garnish oil photo and deleted DMs show both the method and motive: Lena had proof that Brielle’s charity promo was staged.

publicClues.title: Unreleased Music Folder
publicClues.text: At 9:12 PM, the livestream cache points to a hidden folder labeled unreleased event package. The charity scam was connected to media rights.

rounds.title: Round 1: The Photo Alcove
rounds.objective: Review evidence connected to the photo alcove and answer the checkpoint question.
rounds.dialogue: Round 1 is open. This chapter stands on its own, but every clue may also reveal how the larger Black Pelican story is expanding.

rounds.breakingUpdate: Chapter 3 is open: The Charity Mirror. Solve this chapter’s immediate case, but watch for the thread that connects to The Pelican Files.
rounds.liveActionDialogue: Brielle keeps smiling for the crowd until someone asks why her device left the livestream table. The smile stays, but her answer arrives a second too late.

rounds.liveActionDialogue: A guest says the alcove looked “too perfect,” like someone wanted Lena seen there. Brielle replies, “That is what photo moments are for,” but nobody laughs.

rounds.liveActionDialogue: When the deleted DM thread is recovered, Brielle says old messages do not prove intent. Across the room, a staffer whispers, “They prove she knew Lena was asking questions.”

rounds.liveActionTitle: The charity total changes the mood.
rounds.liveActionDialogue: The donation mismatch is read aloud, and the crowd noise fades. Brielle says the numbers were still processing, but her livestream partner refuses to meet her eyes.

rounds.title: Round 5: The Garnish Bottle

rounds.liveActionDialogue: A server identifies the garnish bottle from the prop tray. Brielle says it was decorative, but the detective notes the bottle was handled during the exact window Lena was isolated.

rounds.title: Final Accusation
rounds.objective: Answer the final five questions to close the case.
rounds.dialogue: The final accusation window is open. Choose the culprit, method, motive, key evidence, and the statement that fails.
rounds.countdownReview: The final window is closing. Submit all answers before the case locks.
rounds.breakingUpdate: Final round evidence is live. One detail should help separate the loudest suspect from the provable answer.

openingNarration: Bayou Night Logistics now appears in a charity livestream file. The case becomes harder because the evidence is public-facing: video clips, social posts, deleted messages, and a staged handoff. Solve this chapter by separating what the crowd saw from what the camera actually recorded.
revealScript:
Case closed. This was Level 3: Junior Detective Case. The culprit was Brielle Hart. The method: Lured Lena to the photo alcove and triggered her allergy by switching garnish oil. The motive: To stop Lena from exposing a staged charity livestream scam. The proof: Raw livestream cache, garnish bottle photo, and deleted DM thread.

Continuing Story Thread: Brielle is responsible for this chapter, but the raw livestream cache shows an audio/music rights folder that should not exist. That thread carries into Chapter 4.

seriesArc.nextThread: Chapter 4 follows the sound booth and the stolen media package.

lobbyBackstory: The Pelican Files now move beyond the bar and into the public image of the venue. A staged charity livestream, deleted messages, and a carefully arranged photo moment suggest someone has been turning tragedy into profit. The suspects know the story is getting bigger, and their statements are starting to crack.
